title: Full AI-Powered Environment Management
vision: DotEnvy 2.0 is not just an environment manager — it is an AI copilot for your entire environment lifecycle. It understands your codebase, knows what variables you need before you do, learns from every interaction, and keeps your team in sync automatically.

what_changes_from_1_x.architecture: Complete Railway API v2 with multi-tenant model serving
what_changes_from_1_x.ui: Full webview redesign — modern component-based UI replacing legacy panel
what_changes_from_1_x.ml_model: Production-grade model trained on millions of real-world secrets patterns
breaking_changes: New Railway API v2 — all endpoints versioned under /v2/
New HMAC key format — existing EXTENSION_SHARED_SECRET must be rotated
Webview panel redesigned — custom CSS will not carry over
Config schema updated — .dotenvy.json gets new required fields

pillars.name: AI Environment Copilot
pillars.description: The LLM understands the full project codebase and can set up a complete .env from scratch in under 10 seconds.

features.title: Full Project Scan and Auto-Generate .env
features.description: Analyze every file in the workspace — source code, package.json, Dockerfile, CI config — and generate a complete .env with all required variables, descriptions, and safe placeholder values.

features.title: Variable Explanation in Plain English
features.description: Hover over any variable in .env to see an AI-generated explanation: what service uses it, what it controls, what happens if it is missing or wrong.

features.title: Conflict Detection Across Environments
features.description: Detect when the same variable has inconsistent values across .env.development, .env.staging, and .env.production that could cause environment-specific bugs.

features.title: Predictive Variable Suggestions
features.description: As the developer writes code that uses a new service (e.g. adds stripe import), the extension proactively suggests adding the required Stripe variables before the developer even saves the file.

pillars.name: Continuous Learning Model
pillars.description: The ML model improves from every user interaction across all installations, while keeping all data anonymous.

features.title: Federated Learning from User Feedback
features.description: When users mark secrets as confirmed or false positive across all DotEnvy installations, model improvements are aggregated centrally without sending any secret values — only gradient updates.
features.privacy: Only model weight deltas are sent, never actual secret values

features.title: Model Versioning with A/B Testing
features.description: Run two model versions simultaneously on a percentage split of users. Compare accuracy metrics in real time. Auto-promote the better model after statistical significance is reached.

features.title: Per-Project Model Fine-Tuning
features.description: Teams can fine-tune the model on their specific codebase patterns. A Next.js fintech team gets a model that is better at detecting their specific secret formats.

features.title: Accuracy Dashboard
features.description: Extension settings page showing: current model version, accuracy over time graph, number of confirmed secrets vs false positives, and comparison to baseline model.
features.location: VS Code Settings > DotEnvy > Model Accuracy

pillars.name: Team Intelligence
pillars.description: AI helps entire teams work from consistent, up-to-date environment configurations.

features.title: AI-Powered Onboarding
features.description: New team member clones the repo. DotEnvy scans the codebase, detects the stack, generates a personalized .env setup guide with all required variables and where to get their values.
features.output: Onboarding checklist shown in panel on first open

features.title: Environment Drift Detection
features.description: Periodically compare team members environments via Doppler. Alert when one developer is missing a variable that others have, or has a significantly different value that could cause bugs.

features.title: Smart .env Merge Conflict Resolution
features.description: When two developers modify .env and create a git merge conflict, the extension shows a visual merge tool specifically designed for env files — not a generic text diff.

features.title: Role-Based Variable Visibility
features.description: Admins can mark certain variables as visible only to specific roles (e.g. only senior devs see production database credentials). Junior devs see a placeholder with instructions on how to request access.

pillars.name: Security Intelligence
pillars.description: Move from reactive secret detection to proactive secret prevention.

features.title: Real-Time Risk Scoring as User Types
features.description: As the developer types a string value in code, score it in real time. If it looks like a secret (high entropy, known prefix), show a subtle inline warning before the file is even saved.

features.title: Pre-Commit Secret Prediction
features.description: Enhanced git hook that not only detects known secrets but predicts likely secrets using the ML model — catching novel secret formats that pattern matching would miss.
features.depends_on: v1.0.0 git hooks + v1.5.0 ML model

features.title: GitHub Secret Scanning Integration
features.description: If a secret is pushed to GitHub and GitHub Secret Scanning alerts, DotEnvy receives a webhook notification and immediately shows a prominent warning in VS Code with instructions to rotate the exposed secret.

features.title: Compliance Reports
features.description: Generate compliance-ready reports for SOC2, HIPAA, PCI DSS. Shows: all secrets managed by DotEnvy, encryption methods used, access audit trail, and evidence of secret rotation.

new_infrastructure.description: All endpoints versioned under /v2/. New endpoints: /v2/generate, /v2/explain, /v2/diff-environments, /v2/finetune, /v2/webhook/github-secret-alert. Backward compatible with v1 endpoints until v2.1.
Version-controlled model storage on Railway with automatic rollback. A/B testing infrastructure. Per-project fine-tuned model storage.
Collects anonymized gradient updates from extension installations. Aggregates weekly and releases improved model versions automatically.
Complete redesign using a component-based architecture. Faster rendering, better accessibility, VS Code native theme support. Replaces the legacy panel.js/panel.css.

from_1_x_to_2_0: Run 'dotenvy: Migrate to v2.0' command — auto-updates .dotenvy.json schema
Rotate EXTENSION_SHARED_SECRET — old format not accepted by /v2/ endpoints
Re-run vsce publish after updating package.json to 2.0.0
Update Railway deployment — new service.py with v2 router

success_criteria: Auto-generate accurate .env in under 10 seconds for any project
90%+ secret detection accuracy on held-out test set
Model accuracy measurably improves after 1000 user feedback interactions
Team onboarding time reduced by 80% vs manual .env setup
